subject: [PATCH v6 3/3] iomap: add simple dio path for small direct I/O

reviews.author: changfengnan
reviews.summary: Reviewer changfengnan ran two rounds of tests and found that the original patch caused a 1.5% performance drop in ext4, which they attributed to too many inline functions expanding the filesystem read_iter hot path and increasing frontend/register pressure. They made changes by reverting iomap_dio_rw() and iomap_dio_simple() to non-inline versions, which restored normal performance.

reviews.author: Joanne Koong (author)
reviews.summary: Author acknowledged a concern about patch performance on ext4 and agreed to separate out ext4_iomap_begin into dedicated read and write begins in v3 of the iomap_next series.
reviews.raw_body:
Thanks for tracking this down, I'm glad it doesn't regress the fast
path anymore.

I'll put your name as the author of the patch, especially since you
did all the benchmarking too. I think I just need your legal
permission to add your Signed-off-by. Is that ok? I'll add a
Suggested-by: for christoph since he came up with a lot of the ideas
with IOMAP_DIO_NO_IOMAP_END, moving some of the iteration logic into
iomap_process, etc. I'll carry this in v3 of the iomap_next series.

Thanks,
Joanne

2026-07-08.patch_summary: This patch separates the ext4 iomap begin function into two separate functions: one for read operations and one for write operations. This is done to reduce the size of the ext4_read_iomap_begin function, which was found to be larger than xfs's equivalent function due to being a combined read/write begin function. The separation aims to improve performance by reducing frontend stalls and icache misses. The patch also includes a test suggestion to verify if this change improves ext4 performance.
